$comment: Declared downstream tasks for the RF-Benchmark-Hub leaderboard. The site generator (leaderboard/site/generate.py) renders EVERY task listed here: tasks with valid result JSONs render full leaderboard tables/plots; tasks without results render a WIP card + a minimal WIP page. Canonical task ids, titles, priority (P1/P2/P3) and scope buckets mirror docs/DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md. 'scope' is 'terrestrial_iq' (raw-IQ signal tasks, the board's current focus) or 'csi_sensing' (CSI/channel-domain tasks, a separate out-of-scope track) -- optional, defaults to 'terrestrial_iq' if absent; it drives the site's homepage/sidebar section grouping. 'status' is the declared build state: implemented (has or will have results), wip (task/track defined, baseline pending), planned (blocked / out-of-current-scope, e.g. CSI/channel-domain). Each entry ALSO carries educational content rendered as an explanatory header on the task page: 'description' (one-paragraph what/why), a 'dataset' card ({name, source, n_classes, modality, real_or_synthetic, conditions, license, split}), and metric definitions ('primary_metric' = {name, definition}; 'secondary_metrics' = list of {name, definition}). These educational fields are OPTIONAL: an entry without them renders the same generic header minus the missing pieces. Keep this list in sync with docs/DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md.

tasks.title: Automatic modulation classification
tasks.blurb: Predict the modulation scheme of a raw-IQ window (RadioML 2016.10a, full SNR range). The board's most mature track.
tasks.description: Predict the modulation scheme of a short raw-IQ window (e.g. BPSK, QPSK, QAM, FM). It is the foundational RF classification task and the board's most mature track, used to compare specialized baselines against fine-tuned foundation models.

dataset.name: RadioML 2016.10a
dataset.source: DeepSig / O'Shea et al. (canonical id amc-radioml2016-strat-snr-8010-seed42-v1)
dataset.license: unstated in repo docs (standard community AMC benchmark)
dataset.split: 80/10/10 stratified by (modulation x SNR), seed 42 (no canonical literature split)

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 classification accuracy over the FULL SNR range (all -20..+18 dB pooled, no high-SNR cherry-picking).

secondary_metrics.name: accuracy_vs_snr
secondary_metrics.definition: Accuracy curve reported per SNR bin across the -20..+18 dB grid.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the 11 modulation classes.

tasks.title: Specific emitter identification
tasks.blurb: Identify the transmitting device from hardware imperfections (WiSig), with closed-set / cross-receiver / cross-day tracks.
tasks.description: Identify the specific transmitting device from hardware imperfections (RF fingerprint), predicting emitter identity rather than modulation. It matters for device authentication and spoofing detection, and is reported on separate closed-set / cross-receiver / cross-day tracks.

dataset.name: WiSig (ManyTx)
dataset.source: Hanna, Karunaratne, Cabric, IEEE Access 10:22808-22818, 2022 (DOI 10.1109/ACCESS.2022.3154488; arXiv:2112.15363; data cores.ee.ucla.edu/downloads/datasets/wisig/)
dataset.modality: raw IQ (256,2) — first 256 preamble samples, non-equalized, unit-average-power normalized
dataset.conditions: closed-set / cross-receiver / cross-day tracks reported separately
dataset.split: 80/10/10 stratified by tx, seed 42 (sei-wisig-closed-8010-seed42-v1), plus cross_receiver / cross_day tracks

primary_metric.name: rank1_accuracy
primary_metric.definition: Closed-set top-1 (rank-1) accuracy of predicting the correct transmitting device.

secondary_metrics.name: auroc
secondary_metrics.definition: Open-set area under the ROC curve for detecting unseen/rogue emitters.

secondary_metrics.name: eer
secondary_metrics.definition: Open-set equal-error rate (operating point where false-accept equals false-reject).

tasks.title: Wideband detection
tasks.blurb: Locate and identify signals in time-frequency (RadDet), primary mAP with mAR and IoU.
tasks.description: Locate and identify signals in the time-frequency plane, drawing boxes over a spectrogram to detect where and what signals are present. It matters for wideband spectrum monitoring where many emitters coexist.

dataset.name: RadDet
dataset.source: Huang, Denman, Pemasiri, Martin, Fookes, ICASSP 2025 (arXiv:2501.10407; github.com/abcxyzi/RadDet)
dataset.modality: spectrogram images with YOLO time-frequency box annotations (128/256/512 px resolutions)
dataset.real_or_synthetic: real published (NIST-CBRS captures; synthetic RadDet-1T/9T variants also exist)
dataset.conditions: 6 SNR settings, SNR-averaged; detection vs recognition tracks
dataset.split: RadDet official train/val/test split if provided, else 80/10/10 seed 42 (detect-raddet-<track>-v1)

primary_metric.name: mAP
primary_metric.definition: Mean average precision (COCO-AP) of detected time-frequency boxes across classes.

secondary_metrics.name: mAR
secondary_metrics.definition: Mean average recall across classes over the detection set.

secondary_metrics.name: IoU
secondary_metrics.definition: Intersection-over-union between predicted and ground-truth time-frequency boxes.

tasks.title: Spectrum sensing
tasks.blurb: Per-subband occupancy detection under a target false-alarm rate (DeepSense, pd@pfa=0.1). Track defined; no baseline submitted yet.
tasks.description: Detect per-subband spectrum occupancy (presence/absence of a signal in each frequency subband) under a target false-alarm rate. It matters for dynamic spectrum access and cognitive radio. Track defined; no baseline submitted yet.

dataset.name: DeepSense
dataset.source: Uvaydov, D'Oro, Restuccia, Melodia, IEEE INFOCOM 2021 (DOI 10.1109/INFOCOM42981.2021.9488764; github.com/wineslab/deepsense-spectrum-sensing-datasets)
dataset.conditions: OTA WiFi/LTE-M; occupancy under target pfa
dataset.split: DeepSense official split if provided, else 80/10/10 seed 42 (sensing-deepsense-<split>-v1)

primary_metric.name: pd@pfa=0.1
primary_metric.definition: Probability of detection at a fixed 0.1 false-alarm rate on the occupancy ROC. Note: exact pd@pfa=0.1 not tabulated in the source paper (paper reports precision 98% / recall 97%).

secondary_metrics.name: inference_latency
secondary_metrics.definition: Per-inference latency in milliseconds (paper reports 0.61 ms FPGA in-the-loop).

tasks.title: Interference identification
tasks.blurb: Multi-class jamming / interference recognition (GNSS jamming 6-class IQ). Baseline interf_cnn on the board (0.9987 accuracy).
tasks.description: Recognize the type of interference or jamming affecting a received signal as a multi-class label (e.g. GNSS jamming conditions). It matters for GNSS resilience and jammer characterization. The compact interf_cnn 1-D IQ baseline is on the board.

dataset.name: interf-gnss6
dataset.source: Swinney & Woods 2021, Zenodo record 4629685 (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.4629685); archive Raw_IQ_Dataset.zip ~1.9 GB
dataset.real_or_synthetic: synthetic (MATLAB-generated) but distributed as a ready-to-download raw-IQ archive, treated as public-download

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 classification accuracy over the whole test split.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the 6 interference classes.

tasks.title: Protocol / technology identification
tasks.blurb: Recognise the WiFi 802.11 standard (b/g/n/ax) from raw IQ (T-PRIME). Baseline tprime (config matched to the official code) reproduces the paper: 0.995 accuracy per-window on our within-distribution split, shown alongside the from-paper reference.
tasks.description: Recognize the WiFi 802.11 standard (b/g/n/ax) from raw IQ, identifying the protocol/technology rather than the modulation. It matters for spectrum inventory and standard-aware coexistence.

dataset.name: tprime-wifi4 (T-PRIME)
dataset.source: Genesys Lab / Northeastern (arXiv:2401.04837; github.com/genesys-neu/t-prime; Northeastern DRS neu:h989s847q)
dataset.modality: raw interleaved IQ (2,N) .bin captures at 20 MHz (802.11b upsampled 11->20 MHz)
dataset.conditions: OTA capture at 20 MHz; no SNR grid
dataset.license: unconfirmed / unstated on landing page (DRS collection openly downloadable; only split indices + checksums shipped)
dataset.split: 80/10/10 stratified by protocol class, seed 42 (proto-tprime-wifi4-8010-seed42-v1); repo ships no official split

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 classification accuracy over the whole test split.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the 4 WiFi-standard classes.

tasks.title: SNR / mobility recognition
tasks.blurb: Joint (SNR bin x mobility/Doppler regime) classification on spectrograms (DeepMIMO Phoenix). Spectrogram track; dataset + baseline pending.
tasks.description: Jointly classify a signal into a single combined (SNR bin x mobility/Doppler regime) label from a spectrogram, rather than regressing SNR. It matters for link adaptation and context-aware receivers. Spectrogram track; dataset and baseline pending.

dataset.name: DeepMIMO Phoenix (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (LWM-Spectro Task 2, arXiv:2601.08780); no adopted dataset yet
dataset.n_classes: joint (SNR bin x pedestrian/vehicular) classes — TBD
dataset.conditions: joint SNR-bin x mobility/Doppler regime; TBD

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 accuracy over the joint (SNR, mobility) label space.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the joint label classes.

tasks.title: Beam prediction
tasks.blurb: Best-beam / beam-index prediction from CSI (DeepMIMO). CSI/channel-domain — out of the current terrestrial-IQ scope; separate CSI track.
tasks.description: Predict the best beam / beam index from a predefined mmWave codebook given channel/CSI input (e.g. sub-6 GHz CSI to strongest mmWave beam). It matters for reducing beam-alignment overhead in 5G/6G. CSI/channel-domain, out of the current terrestrial-IQ scope; separate CSI track.

dataset.name: DeepMIMO (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (a city scenario held out from pretraining); no adopted dataset yet
dataset.n_classes: codebook size fixed per scenario manifest — TBD

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 accuracy of predicting the best beam index.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the beam codebook.

tasks.title: Direction finding
tasks.blurb: Angle-of-arrival estimation from a multi-antenna array. Blocked on a public multi-rx dataset (both FM evals use private OTA testbeds).
tasks.description: Estimate the angle-of-arrival (direction-of-arrival) of a signal from spatial phase across a multi-antenna array, as discrete angle-bin classification or angle regression. It matters for localization and emitter tracking. Blocked on a public multi-rx dataset (both FM evals use private OTA testbeds).

dataset.name: TBD / proposed (no public canonical AoA dataset)
dataset.source: DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md notes both FM evals (WirelessJEPA, IQFM) are on private OTA MIMO testbeds; needs a public multi-rx dataset first
dataset.n_classes: discrete angle bins (e.g. 225 x 10-deg bins on IQFM testbed) — TBD
dataset.real_or_synthetic: TBD (private OTA MIMO testbeds in cited work)
dataset.conditions: multi-antenna array; angle-bin classification or angular-error regression

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 accuracy over discrete angle bins (angle-bin classification).

secondary_metrics.name: mean_angular_error
secondary_metrics.definition: Mean angular error in degrees if a regression head is used (lower is better).

tasks.title: Line-of-sight / NLOS
tasks.blurb: Line-of-sight vs non-line-of-sight classification from CSI (DeepMIMO). CSI/channel-domain — out of current scope; separate CSI track.
tasks.description: Classify a link as line-of-sight vs non-line-of-sight from channel/CSI embeddings. It matters for reliable ranging, positioning and channel-aware scheduling. CSI/channel-domain, out of current scope; separate CSI track.

dataset.name: DeepMIMO (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (held-out city, e.g. Denver densified); no adopted dataset yet
dataset.conditions: held-out city scenario; limited-label regime in cited FM evals

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 accuracy of the LoS vs NLoS binary decision.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over LoS/NLoS (both cited FM papers use F1).

tasks.title: Positioning
tasks.blurb: Localization / geolocation from CSI (5G NR, mean positioning error in metres). CSI-domain + needs a regression-metric protocol entry.
tasks.description: Estimate transmitter/user 2D-3D coordinates (localization/geolocation) from CSI as a regression to physical location. It matters for indoor/outdoor positioning without GNSS. CSI-domain and needs a regression-metric protocol entry.

dataset.name: 5G NR CSI positioning set (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (DeepMIMO outdoor, or the WavesFM real 476-location set if released); no adopted dataset yet
dataset.real_or_synthetic: synthetic (DeepMIMO outdoor) or real (WavesFM set) — TBD

primary_metric.name: mean_positioning_error
primary_metric.definition: Mean positioning error in meters (lower is better) — a regression metric requiring a regression-metric entry in the protocol.

tasks.title: Human activity recognition
tasks.blurb: Human activity recognition from WiFi CSI (6-class). CSI-sensing domain — out of the current signal scope; separate CSI / RF-sensing track.
tasks.description: Recognize human activities (e.g. 6 motion classes) from WiFi CSI measurements. It matters for device-free RF sensing applications like fall detection and smart-home monitoring. CSI-sensing domain, out of the current signal scope; separate CSI / RF-sensing track.

dataset.name: WiFi-CSI HAR set (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (public 6-class HSD-style WiFi-CSI HAR set); no adopted dataset yet

primary_metric.name: accuracy_overall
primary_metric.definition: Top-1 accuracy over the human-activity classes.

secondary_metrics.name: macro_f1
secondary_metrics.definition: Unweighted mean of per-class F1 over the activity classes.

tasks.title: Channel estimation
tasks.blurb: MIMO-OFDM channel estimation (regression to MSE). CSI/channel-domain + needs a regression-metric protocol entry; separate CSI track.
tasks.description: Estimate the MIMO-OFDM channel response between user and base station, as a regression whose quality is scored by MSE. It matters for receiver performance and CSI feedback/compression in massive-MIMO systems. CSI/channel-domain and needs a regression-metric protocol entry; separate CSI track.

dataset.name: MIMO-OFDM channel set (TBD / proposed)
dataset.source: recommended in DOWNSTREAM_TASKS.md (WavesFM: simulated uplink MIMO-OFDM, 16-antenna BS, 3GPP UMi); no adopted dataset yet

primary_metric.name: MSE
primary_metric.definition: Mean squared error between estimated and true channel response (lower is better); requires a regression-metric entry in the protocol.
